records.ety: [L. revolvere, revolutum; pref. re- re- + volvere to roll, turn round. See Voluble, and cf. Revolt, revolution.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Revolved(?); p. pr. & vb. n. Revolving.]

defs: To turn or roll round on, or as on, an axis, like a wheel; to rotate, -- which is the more specific word in this sense.
quotes: If the earth revolve thus, each house near the equator must move a thousand miles an hour.

defs: To move in a curved path round a center; as, the planets revolve round the sun.
To pass in cycles; as, the centuries revolve.
To cause to turn, as on an axis.
quotes: Then in the east her turn she shines, Revolved on heaven's great axile.

defs: Hence, to turn over and over in the mind; to reflect repeatedly upon; to consider all aspects of.
quotes: This having heard, straight I again revolved The law and prophets.
